req.title: Create patient (consent)
req.statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected. Consent state at the time of the operation must be tested, including post-revocation timing windows.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers patient resource creation by a clinician via POST /Patient. We must test: (1) a valid creation with all mandatory demographics (EP positive), (2) rejection when a mandatory element is absent (NEG/DT negative), (3) a boundary case on a field length or consent timing window (BVA boundary), (4) consent revocation state transition (STC boundary). Five tests are provided to satisfy the minimum of three while covering all heuristic categories cited in the requirement. Each test uses a shared 'clinician_token' fixture and a 'fhir_client' fixture that wraps requests.Session with base URL and auth header. Idempotency is achieved by deleting any created resource in teardown via the returned Location header.

tests.name: test_create_patient_all_mandatory_fields_returns_201
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Active consent record exists for the patient identity
actions: Build a Patient FHIR payload containing all mandatory elements: resourceType, name (family+given), gender, birthDate
POST payload to /Patient with Authorization: Bearer <clinician_token>
Capture response status and Location header
expected: Location header references newly created Patient resource
Response body contains an id field
trace: R-HLT-101: registered clinician creates patient resource with validated demographics
R-HLT-101: patient resource stored on success

tests.name: test_create_patient_missing_birthdate_returns_422
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Build a Patient FHIR payload that omits the mandatory 'birthDate' field
POST payload to /Patient with Authorization: Bearer <clinician_token>
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message referencing 'birthDate'
trace: R-HLT-101: creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected
R-HLT-101: error pathway 422 when mandatory element missing
tests.executable:
def test_create_patient_missing_birthdate_returns_422(fhir_client, clinician_token):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "name": [{"family": "Smith", "given": ["John"]}],
        "gender": "male"
        # birthDate intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = fhir_client.post(
        "/Patient",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    # Server must surface a diagnostic referencing the missing field
    body_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "birthdate" in body_text or "birth_date" in body_text or "mandatory" in body_text, \
        f"Response should mention missing field, got: {body}"

tests.name: test_create_patient_family_name_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
System imposes a maximum family-name length (assumed 64 characters per spec)
actions: Build a Patient payload with family name exactly 64 characters long (max allowed boundary)
POST payload to /Patient with Authorization: Bearer <clinician_token>
Record status; if 201 clean up; assert acceptance
expected: HTTP 201 Created — exactly-64-char name is at the valid boundary and must be accepted
Patient resource id returned in response body
trace: R-HLT-101: validated demographics must accept values at field length boundary
BVA on maximum allowed length of family name element

tests.name: test_create_patient_after_consent_revocation_is_rejected
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
A consent record exists that can be revoked via <revoke_consent> helper
System enforces consent state before allowing patient creation
actions: Revoke consent for the test subject identity using <revoke_consent> fixture
Wait for revocation propagation using <wait_for_consent_propagation> helper (timing window)
Attempt POST /Patient with full mandatory payload
expected: HTTP 403 Forbidden or 422 Unprocessable Entity — system must reject creation after consent revocation
Response body indicates consent or authorization failure
trace: R-HLT-101: consent state at time of operation must be tested
STC: Active-consent -> Consent-revoked state transition coverage

tests.name: test_create_patient_decision_table_gender_values
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician with a valid bearer token
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
FHIR R4 restricts gender to male|female|other|unknown
actions: Iterate over invalid gender value 'nonbinary' (outside FHIR ValueSet)
POST Patient payload with that invalid gender to /Patient
Assert rejection; separately POST with valid gender 'other' and assert acceptance
trace: R-HLT-101: validated demographics — gender field must conform to FHIR ValueSet
DT: cartesian coverage of valid vs invalid gender column
